name: Jacques Vallée
summary: French-American scientist, author, and long-term UAP researcher whose work broadened inquiry beyond a purely extraterrestrial interpretation.

relationships.context: Author of the foundational 1965 scientific appraisal
Author of the influential 1969 comparative study
Served in the NIDS scientific advisory network

profileSummary: French-American scientist, author, and long-term UAP researcher whose books and investigations have shaped modern thinking about UAP.
background: Astronomer, computer scientist, author, and technology investor with decades of research into UFO and UAP reports.
inclusionNote: Included because his research, publications, and collaboration with J. Allen Hynek materially influenced the conceptual and scientific history of UAP investigation.
